assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements under IFRS.
The intragroup dividend has resulted in a recognised intercompany receivable/payable position in consolidation.
The issue is limited to accounting models evidenced in the provided IAS 21 and IFRS 9 context.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to a risk that could affect profit or loss.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a separate hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge accounting for a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk.

approaches.label: Foreign currency accounting without hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 provides a standalone accounting model for exchange differences on foreign currency monetary items, including intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements.
